title: Classifying a triangle by its sides
problem: A triangle has sides of length 7 cm, 7 cm, and 10 cm. Classify this triangle by its sides.

steps.instruction: Count how many sides are equal.
steps.working: Two sides are 7 cm. The third side is 10 cm.

steps.instruction: Recall the classification rules for sides.
steps.working: Equilateral: all three sides equal. Isosceles: exactly two sides equal. Scalene: no sides equal.
Exactly two sides are equal, so the triangle is isosceles.

title: Finding a missing angle in a triangle
problem: Two angles of a triangle are $72°$ and $58°$. Find the third angle.

steps.instruction: Recall the angle sum of a triangle.
steps.working: The angles of any triangle add to $180°$.

title: Finding angles from a ratio
problem: The three angles of a triangle are in the ratio $2:3:7$. Find the size of each angle.

steps.working: These represent the three angles in the given ratio.

steps.instruction: Use the angle sum of a triangle.
